entities.name: MILLET
entities.description: The cereal crop studied for nutrient use efficiency and grain yield.

entities.name: NUTRIENT USE EFFICIENCY
entities.description: The efficiency with which the crop utilizes nutrients from the soil and fertilizers.

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: The amount of grain produced per unit area.

entities.name: ZAÏ TECHNIQUE
entities.description: A traditional planting pit technique used to concentrate water and nutrients.

entities.name: CATTLE MANURE
entities.description: Organic fertilizer derived from cattle waste used to provide nutrients.

entities.name: MINERAL FERTILIZER
entities.description: Inorganic fertilizer containing nitrogen, phosphorus, and potassium.

entities.name: NITROGEN
entities.description: A primary plant nutrient essential for growth.

entities.name: PHOSPHORUS
entities.description: A primary plant nutrient essential for root and seed development.

entities.name: POTASSIUM
entities.description: A primary plant nutrient essential for overall plant health.

entities.name: PLINTHOSOLS
entities.description: A type of soil characterized by iron-rich layers.

entities.name: NIGER
entities.description: The country where the field experiment was conducted.

entities.name: SADORÉ
entities.description: The location of the ICRISAT research station in Niger.

entities.name: MANURE DECOMPOSITION
entities.description: The process by which organic matter in manure is broken down, releasing nutrients.

entities.name: N UTILIZATION EFFICIENCY
entities.description: The specific efficiency of nitrogen uptake and use by the crop.
